heading: Operation
label: 7482 Operation
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)441210:442 (47 words after 1st semicolon, and 72 words before 3d semicolon, of 1st sentence; and last sentence).(47 words after 1st semicolon, and 72 words before 3d semicolon, of 1st sentence; and last sentence); added , subch. I, § 34 (47 words after 1st semicolon, and 72 words before 3d semicolon, of 1st sentence; and last sentence of last par.), .June 3, 1916, ch. 134, § 47dJune 4, 1920, ch. 22741 Stat. 779The word “supplies” is substituted for the words “such arms, ammunition, accoutrements, equipments, tentage, field equipage”, since, under the definition of the word “supplies”, in , those words are covered by the word “supplies”. The words “belonging to the United States”, “and imparting military instruction and training thereat”, “during the period of their attendance”, “theoretical and practical instruction”, “persons attending the camps authorized by this section”, and “as he may deem” are omitted as surplusage. The word “detail” is substituted for the word “employ”. The word “members” is substituted for the words “officers, warrant officers, and enlisted men”.section 101(26) of this title
Amendments2018—, substituted “section 7481” for “section 4411” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–232, § 809(a), renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232, § 808(c)(2)section 4412 of this title

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

children.text: prescribe the periods during which they will be operated;
prescribe the courses to be taught;
detail members of the Regular Army to designated duties relating to the camps;
furnish uniforms, subsistence, and medical attendance and supplies to persons attending the camps; and
